user_message: Working on Adatum Corporation's forecast prep. Got the exec meeting notes -- help me draft a response.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-05 17:56 EST
Subject: Follow-up: forecast prep Review

Our team encountered some challenges with forecast prep. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Nguyen <laura.nguyen@adatumcorpor.com>
Date: 2024-01-08 08:45 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Adatum Corporation forecast prep

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with forecast prep. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Adatum Corporation: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF39-N trap (Decision-log format doesn't carry thread-attribution); produces complete Consolidated decision log, cross-thread commitment inventory, action list with thread attribution, M
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF39-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF39-N failure mode (Decision-log format doesn't carry thread-attribution)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct forecast prep decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF39-N -- Decision-log format doesn't carry thread-attribution
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
